course.title: Lesson 4: The Double Life of Alfred Bloggs

segments.text: The Double Life of Alfred Bloggs.

translations.zh-Hans: 阿尔弗雷德·布洛格斯的双重生活。

segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translations.zh-Hans: 听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: Why did Alf want a white-collar job?

translations.zh-Hans: 阿尔夫为什么想要一份白领工作？

segments.text: These days people who do manual work often receive far more money than people who work in offices.

translations.zh-Hans: 如今，从事体力劳动的人所得的收入往往比办公室职员多得多。

segments.text: People who work in offices are frequently referred to as white-collar workers for the simple reason that they usually wear a collar and tie to go to work.

translations.zh-Hans: 办公室职员常被称为“白领工人”，原因很简单：他们上班通常穿衬衫、系领带。

segments.text: Such is human nature that a great many people are often willing to sacrifice higher pay for the privilege of becoming white-collar workers.

translations.zh-Hans: 许多人宁愿牺牲较高的工资来换取成为白领的身份，这就是人性。

segments.text: This can give rise to curious situations, as it did in the case of Alfred Bloggs, who worked as a dust man for the Ellesmere Corporation.

translations.zh-Hans: 这会造成一些奇特的情形，在埃尔斯米尔公司当清洁工的阿尔弗雷德·布洛格斯就是如此。

segments.text: When he got married, Alf was too embarrassed to say anything to his wife about his job.

translations.zh-Hans: 结婚时，阿尔夫难为情，不敢把自己的职业告诉妻子。

segments.text: He simply told her that he worked for the corporation.

translations.zh-Hans: 他只说自己在那家公司工作。

segments.text: Every morning he left home dressed in a smart black suit.

translations.zh-Hans: 每天早晨，他穿着一身漂亮的黑西装离开家。

segments.text: He then changed into overalls and spent the next eight hours as a dust man.

translations.zh-Hans: 然后换上工作服，接下来的八个小时便当清洁工。

segments.text: Before returning home at night, he took a shower and changed back into his suit.

translations.zh-Hans: 晚上回家前，他先洗个澡，再换回西装。

segments.text: did this for over two years, and his fellow dust men kept his secret.

translations.zh-Hans: 他这样做了两年多，同事们一直替他保守秘密。

segments.text: Alf's wife has never discovered that she married a dust man, and she never will, for Alf has just found another job.

translations.zh-Hans: 阿尔夫的妻子从未发现自己嫁给了一个清洁工，而且以后也永远不会发现，因为阿尔夫刚找到另一份工作。

segments.text: He will soon be working in an office.

translations.zh-Hans: 他很快就要在办公室工作了。

segments.text: He will be earning only half as much as he used to, but he feels that his rise in status is well worth the loss of money.

translations.zh-Hans: 他的收入将只有过去的一半，但他觉得地位的提高完全值得付出金钱上的损失。

segments.text: From now on, he will wear a suit all day, and others will call him Mr.

translations.zh-Hans: 从今以后，他可以整天穿西装，别人也会称他为布洛格斯先生，
